title: Avoid Throwing Exceptions in Finally Blocks
description: Throwing an exception in a finally block can override an existing exception from the try or catch block. This can result in the loss of the original exception's message and stack trace, complicating the diagnosis and troubleshooting process.
recommendation: Avoid throwing exceptions in finally blocks. Instead, ensure to handle exceptions appropriately within the try or catch blocks.
impact: Neglecting this rule can mask the original exception, making it challenging to diagnose and troubleshoot the root cause of a problem.

checks.message: Avoid throwing exceptions in finally blocks; masks the original exception
